LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FILED HIS OPPOSITION THIS MORNING. THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, DMV DIRECTOR GEORGE VALVERDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A VIABLE FEDERAL DUE PROCESS CLAIM. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE COMPLIANCE WITH THE TORT CLAIMS ACT (SEE G.C. SECTION 800 ET SEQ) AND MUST HAVE FILED A CLAIM BEFORE FILING HIS LAWSUIT (SEE G.C. SECTION 911.2 AND 945.4. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE A STATUTORY BASIS OF LIABILITY AGAINST DEFENDANTS. (G.C. SECTION 815, 815.2) THERE IS NO COMMON-LAW LIABILITY AGAINST THE STATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY AND STEVE HEMINGER, BATA EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Plaintiff has not complied with the claims requirement of the Government Claims Act, and there is no common-law liability against BATA. Plaintiff's claim is now time-barred. No viable claim is alleged. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brett Lueder, plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona. No other appearances on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY, STEVE HEMINGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar. On August 17, 2010, court gave plaintiff leave to amend. Defendant to respond to amended pleading when filed. Order on demurrer heard on August 17, 2010 signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bret Lueder (530.845.6571) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona

No other appearances. Plaintiff filed his opposition this morning. The matter was not argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, DMV DIRECTOR GEORGE VALVERDE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Plaintiff must plead a viable federal due process claim. Plaintiff must allege compliance with the Tort Claims Act (see G.C. section 800 et seq) and must have filed a claim before filing his lawsuit (see G.C. section 911.2 and 945.4. Plaintiff must allege a statutory basis of liability against defendants. (G.C. section 815, 815.2) There is no common-law liability against the state. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Bret Lueder appearing in Pro Per, informs the Court that a first amended complaint has been filed. The Court vacates its tentative ruling and rules as follows: Demurrer is Off Calendar, first amended complaint filed. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
